title: OmegaUse: Building a General-Purpose GUI Agent for Autonomous Task Execution
summary: Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents show great potential for enabling foundation models to complete real-world tasks, revolutionizing human-computer interaction and improving human productivity. In this report, we present OmegaUse, a general-purpose GUI agent model for autonomous task execution on both mobile and desktop platforms, supporting computer-use and phone-use scenarios. Building an effective GUI agent model relies on two factors: (1) high-quality data and (2) effective training methods. To address these, we introduce a carefully engineered data-construction pipeline and a decoupled training paradigm. For data construction, we leverage rigorously curated open-source datasets and introduce a novel automated synthesis framework that integrates bottom-up autonomous exploration with top-down taxonomy-guided generation to create high-fidelity synthetic data. For training, to better leverage these data, we adopt a two-stage strategy: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) to establish fundamental interaction syntax, followed by Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to improve spatial grounding and sequential planning. To balance computational efficiency with agentic reasoning capacity, OmegaUse is built on a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) backbone. To evaluate cross-terminal capabilities in an offline setting, we introduce OS-Nav, a benchmark suite spanning multiple operating systems: ChiM-Nav, targeting Chinese Android mobile environments, and Ubu-Nav, focusing on routine desktop interactions on Ubuntu. Extensive experiments show that OmegaUse is highly competitive across established GUI benchmarks, achieving a state-of-the-art (SOTA) score of 96.3% on ScreenSpot-V2 and a leading 79.1% step success rate on AndroidControl. OmegaUse also performs strongly on OS-Nav, reaching 74.24% step success on ChiM-Nav and 55.9% average success on Ubu-Nav.
tldr: The paper introduces OmegaUse, a general-purpose GUI agent leveraging a novel data construction pipeline and a decoupled training paradigm (SFT+GRPO) with a MoE backbone, achieving SOTA results on various GUI benchmarks and a newly proposed cross-terminal benchmark OS-Nav.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了OmegaUse，一种通用的GUI代理，它利用新颖的数据构建流程和解耦的训练模式（SFT+GRPO），并使用MoE骨干网络，在各种GUI基准测试和新提出的跨终端基准测试OS-Nav上实现了SOTA结果。
summary_zh: 图形用户界面 (GUI) 交互代理在使基础模型完成实际任务方面显示出巨大潜力，从而彻底改变人机交互并提高人类生产力。在本报告中，我们介绍OmegaUse，一种通用的GUI交互代理模型，用于在移动和桌面平台上的自主任务执行，支持计算机使用和手机使用场景。构建高效的GUI交互代理模型依赖两个因素：（1）高质量的数据和（2）有效的训练方法。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了一个精心设计的数据构建流程和一个解耦的训练范式。在数据构建方面，我们利用严格管理的高质量开源数据集，并引入了一种新颖的自动化合成框架，该框架集成了自下而上的自主探索和自上而下的分类法引导生成，以创建高保真度的合成数据。在训练方面，为了更好地利用这些数据，我们采用两阶段策略：首先进行监督微调 (SFT) 以建立基本的交互语法，然后进行分组相对策略优化 (GRPO) 以改善空间定位和顺序规划。为了平衡计算效率与代理推理能力，OmegaUse构建在混合专家 (MoE) 主干网络之上。为了在离线环境中评估跨终端能力，我们引入了OS-Nav，这是一套跨多个操作系统的基准测试套件：ChiM-Nav，以中国Android移动环境为目标，以及Ubu-Nav，侧重于Ubuntu上的日常桌面交互。大量实验表明，OmegaUse在已建立的GUI基准测试中具有很强的竞争力，在ScreenSpot-V2上实现了96.3%的最先进 (SOTA) 分数，并在AndroidControl上实现了领先的79.1%步成功率。OmegaUse在OS-Nav上也有出色的表现，在ChiM-Nav上达到了74.24%的步成功率，在Ubu-Nav上达到了55.9%的平均成功率。

title: Advancing Open-source World Models
summary: We present LingBot-World, an open-sourced world simulator stemming from video generation. Positioned as a top-tier world model, LingBot-World offers the following features. (1) It maintains high fidelity and robust dynamics in a broad spectrum of environments, including realism, scientific contexts, cartoon styles, and beyond. (2) It enables a minute-level horizon while preserving contextual consistency over time, which is also known as "long-term memory". (3) It supports real-time interactivity, achieving a latency of under 1 second when producing 16 frames per second. We provide public access to the code and model in an effort to narrow the divide between open-source and closed-source technologies. We believe our release will empower the community with practical applications across areas like content creation, gaming, and robot learning.
tldr: LingBot-World, an open-sourced world model built upon video generation, offers high fidelity, long-term contextual consistency, and real-time interactivity across diverse environments, aiming to bridge the gap between open and closed-source technologies.
tldr_zh: LingBot-World是一个开源的世界模型，基于视频生成技术，在多种环境中提供高保真度、长期上下文一致性和实时交互性，旨在弥合开源和闭源技术之间的差距。
summary_zh: 我们提出了LingBot-World，一个源于视频生成的开源世界模拟器。 LingBot-World定位为顶级的世界模型，具有以下特性。(1) 它在广泛的环境中保持高保真度和鲁棒的动力学特性，包括现实环境、科学情境、卡通风格及其他。(2) 它支持分钟级的时间跨度，同时保持上下文在时间上的一致性，即所谓的“长期记忆”。(3) 它支持实时交互，在每秒生成16帧的情况下，延迟低于1秒。 我们公开提供代码和模型，旨在缩小开源和闭源技术之间的差距。 我们相信我们的发布将为社区赋能，使其在内容创作、游戏和机器人学习等领域实现实践应用。

title: One Step Is Enough: Dispersive MeanFlow Policy Optimization
summary:
Real-time robotic control demands fast action generation. However, existing generative policies based on diffusion and flow matching require multi-step
  sampling, fundamentally limiting deployment in time-critical scenarios. We propose Dispersive MeanFlow Policy Optimization (DMPO), a unified framework that
  enables true one-step generation through three key components: MeanFlow for mathematically-derived single-step inference without knowledge distillation,
  dispersive regularization to prevent representation collapse, and reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuning to surpass expert demonstrations. Experiments
  across RoboMimic manipulation and OpenAI Gym locomotion benchmarks demonstrate competitive or superior performance compared to multi-step baselines. With
  our lightweight model architecture and the three key algorithmic components working in synergy, DMPO exceeds real-time control requirements (>120Hz) with
  5-20x inference speedup, reaching hundreds of Hertz on high-performance GPUs. Physical deployment on a Franka-Emika-Panda robot validates real-world
  applicability.
tldr: This paper introduces Dispersive MeanFlow Policy Optimization (DMPO), a novel one-step generation method for real-time robotic control using reinforcement learning, achieving significant speedups compared to multi-step approaches and demonstrating real-world applicability.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为分散均值流策略优化(DMPO)的新型单步生成方法，该方法利用强化学习进行实时机器人控制，与多步方法相比，实现了显著的加速，并展示了实际应用。
summary_zh: 实时机器人控制需要快速的动作生成。然而，现有的基于扩散和流匹配的生成策略需要多步采样，从根本上限制了其在时间关键型场景中的部署。我们提出了离散平均流策略优化（DMPO），一种统一框架，可以通过三个关键组件实现真正的单步生成：用于数学推导的单步推理且无需知识蒸馏的平均流，防止表征崩溃的离散正则化，以及超越专家演示的强化学习（RL）微调。在RoboMimic操作和OpenAI Gym运动基准测试中的实验表明，与多步基线相比，该方法具有竞争力或更优越的性能。凭借我们轻量级的模型架构以及协同工作的三大关键算法组件，DMPO超越了实时控制的要求(>120Hz)，实现了5-20倍的推理加速，在高性能GPU上达到了数百赫兹。在Franka-Emika-Panda机器人上的物理部署验证了其在现实世界中的适用性。

title: MeCo: Enhancing LLM-Empowered Multi-Robot Collaboration via Similar Task Memoization
summary: Multi-robot systems have been widely deployed in real-world applications, providing significant improvements in efficiency and reductions in labor costs. However, most existing multi-robot collaboration methods rely on extensive task-specific training, which limits their adaptability to new or diverse scenarios. Recent research leverages the language understanding and reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) to enable more flexible collaboration without specialized training. Yet, current LLM-empowered approaches remain inefficient: when confronted with identical or similar tasks, they must replan from scratch because they omit task-level similarities. To address this limitation, we propose MeCo, a similarity-aware multi-robot collaboration framework that applies the principle of ``cache and reuse'' (a.k.a., memoization) to reduce redundant computation. Unlike simple task repetition, identifying and reusing solutions for similar but not identical tasks is far more challenging, particularly in multi-robot settings. To this end, MeCo introduces a new similarity testing method that retrieves previously solved tasks with high relevance, enabling effective plan reuse without re-invoking LLMs. Furthermore, we present MeCoBench, the first benchmark designed to evaluate performance on similar-task collaboration scenarios. Experimental results show that MeCo substantially reduces planning costs and improves success rates compared with state-of-the-art approaches.
tldr: The paper introduces MeCo, a framework that enhances LLM-empowered multi-robot collaboration by memoizing and reusing solutions for similar tasks, improving efficiency and success rates. It also introduces MeCoBench for evaluating similar-task collaboration.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MeCo，一个通过记忆和重用相似任务的解决方案来增强LLM赋能的多机器人协作框架，从而提高效率和成功率。它还介绍了用于评估类似任务协作的MeCoBench。
summary_zh: 多机器人系统已广泛部署于实际应用中，在效率提升和劳动力成本降低方面带来了显著改善。然而，现有的大多数多机器人协作方法依赖于大量的特定任务训练，这限制了它们对新场景或多样化场景的适应性。最近的研究利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 的语言理解和推理能力，从而能够在无需专门训练的情况下实现更灵活的协作。然而，目前基于LLM的方法仍然效率低下：当面临相同或相似的任务时，它们必须从头开始重新规划，因为它们忽略了任务层面的相似性。为了解决这一局限性，我们提出了一种相似性感知的多机器人协作框架 MeCo，该框架应用“缓存和重用”（又称备忘录化）的原则来减少冗余计算。与简单的任务重复不同，识别和重用相似但不完全相同的任务的解决方案更具挑战性，尤其是在多机器人环境中。为此，MeCo 引入了一种新的相似性测试方法，可以检索先前解决且具有高度相关性的任务，从而无需重新调用 LLM 即可实现有效的方案重用。此外，我们提出了 MeCoBench，这是第一个旨在评估类似任务协作场景性能的基准。实验结果表明，与最先进的方法相比，MeCo 显著降低了规划成本并提高了成功率。

title: Vibro-Sense: Robust Vibration-based Impulse Response Localization and Trajectory Tracking for Robotic Hands
summary: Rich contact perception is crucial for robotic manipulation, yet traditional tactile skins remain expensive and complex to integrate. This paper presents a scalable alternative: high-accuracy whole-body touch localization via vibro-acoustic sensing. By equipping a robotic hand with seven low-cost piezoelectric microphones and leveraging an Audio Spectrogram Transformer, we decode the vibrational signatures generated during physical interaction. Extensive evaluation across stationary and dynamic tasks reveals a localization error of under 5 mm in static conditions. Furthermore, our analysis highlights the distinct influence of material properties: stiff materials (e.g., metal) excel in impulse response localization due to sharp, high-bandwidth responses, whereas textured materials (e.g., wood) provide superior friction-based features for trajectory tracking. The system demonstrates robustness to the robot's own motion, maintaining effective tracking even during active operation. Our primary contribution is demonstrating that complex physical contact dynamics can be effectively decoded from simple vibrational signals, offering a viable pathway to widespread, affordable contact perception in robotics. To accelerate research, we provide our full datasets, models, and experimental setups as open-source resources.
tldr: The paper presents a low-cost, high-accuracy vibro-acoustic sensing system for robotic hands that enables touch localization and trajectory tracking by decoding vibrational signatures. They open-source their datasets, models, and setups.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种低成本、高精度的机器人手振动声学传感系统，通过解码振动信号实现触摸定位和轨迹跟踪。他们开源了他们的数据集、模型和实验装置。
summary_zh: 丰富的触觉感知对于机器人操作至关重要，然而传统的触觉皮肤仍然价格昂贵且集成复杂。本文提出了一种可扩展的替代方案：通过振动声学传感实现高精度全身触摸定位。我们通过在机械手上装备七个低成本压电麦克风，并利用音频频谱图转换器解码物理交互过程中产生的振动特征。在静态和动态任务中的广泛评估表明，在静态条件下定位误差小于5毫米。 此外，我们的分析突出了材料属性的独特影响：刚性材料（例如，金属）由于其尖锐、高带宽的响应，在冲击响应定位方面表现出色；而纹理材料（例如，木材）为轨迹跟踪提供了卓越的基于摩擦力的特征。该系统对机器人自身的运动具有鲁棒性，即使在主动操作期间也能保持有效的跟踪。 我们的主要贡献是证明了可以从简单的振动信号中有效地解码复杂的物理接触动力学，为机器人技术中广泛、经济的触觉感知提供了一条可行的途径。为了加速研究，我们将完整的 datasets, models 和实验装置作为开源资源提供。

title: STORM: Slot-based Task-aware Object-centric Representation for robotic Manipulation
summary: Visual foundation models provide strong perceptual features for robotics, but their dense representations lack explicit object-level structure, limiting robustness and contractility in manipulation tasks. We propose STORM (Slot-based Task-aware Object-centric Representation for robotic Manipulation), a lightweight object-centric adaptation module that augments frozen visual foundation models with a small set of semantic-aware slots for robotic manipulation. Rather than retraining large backbones, STORM employs a multi-phase training strategy: object-centric slots are first stabilized through visual--semantic pretraining using language embeddings, then jointly adapted with a downstream manipulation policy. This staged learning prevents degenerate slot formation and preserves semantic consistency while aligning perception with task objectives. Experiments on object discovery benchmarks and simulated manipulation tasks show that STORM improves generalization to visual distractors, and control performance compared to directly using frozen foundation model features or training object-centric representations end-to-end. Our results highlight multi-phase adaptation as an efficient mechanism for transforming generic foundation model features into task-aware object-centric representations for robotic control.
tldr: The paper introduces STORM, a lightweight module that adapts frozen visual foundation models into task-aware, object-centric representations using a multi-phase training strategy for improved robotic manipulation and generalization.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为STORM的轻量级模块，该模块使用多阶段训练策略将冻结的视觉基础模型调整为具有任务意识、以对象为中心的表示，从而改进机器人操作和泛化能力。
summary_zh: 视觉基础模型为机器人技术提供了强大的感知特征，但其密集表示缺乏明确的物体级别结构，限制了操作任务中的鲁棒性和可控性。我们提出了STORM (基于槽位的任务感知、以物体为中心的机器人操作表示)，这是一个轻量级的物体中心适配模块，它通过一小部分语义感知的槽位来增强冻结的视觉基础模型，用于机器人操作。STORM没有重新训练大型骨干网络，而是采用了一种多阶段训练策略：首先通过使用语言嵌入的视觉-语义预训练来稳定物体中心槽位，然后将其与下游操作策略联合适配。这种分阶段的学习方法可以防止退化的槽位形成，并保持语义一致性，同时将感知与任务目标对齐。在物体发现基准和模拟操作任务上的实验表明，与直接使用冻结的基础模型特征或端到端训练以物体为中心的表示相比，STORM提高了对视觉干扰的泛化能力，并提升了控制性能。我们的结果表明，多阶段适配是一种有效的机制，可以将通用基础模型特征转化为用于机器人控制的任务感知、以物体为中心的表示。

title: Demonstration-Free Robotic Control via LLM Agents
summary: Robotic manipulation has increasingly adopted vision-language-action (VLA) models, which achieve strong performance but typically require task-specific demonstrations and fine-tuning, and often generalize poorly under domain shift. We investigate whether general-purpose large language model (LLM) agent frameworks, originally developed for software engineering, can serve as an alternative control paradigm for embodied manipulation. We introduce FAEA (Frontier Agent as Embodied Agent), which applies an LLM agent framework directly to embodied manipulation without modification. Using the same iterative reasoning that enables software agents to debug code, FAEA enables embodied agents to reason through manipulation strategies. We evaluate an unmodified frontier agent, Claude Agent SDK, across the LIBERO, ManiSkill3, and MetaWorld benchmarks. With privileged environment state access, FAEA achieves success rates of 84.9%, 85.7%, and 96%, respectively. This level of task success approaches that of VLA models trained with less than 100 demonstrations per task, without requiring demonstrations or fine-tuning. With one round of human feedback as an optional optimization, performance increases to 88.2% on LIBERO. This demonstration-free capability has immediate practical value: FAEA can autonomously explore novel scenarios in simulation and generate successful trajectories for training data augmentation in embodied learning. Our results indicate that general-purpose agents are sufficient for a class of manipulation tasks dominated by deliberative, task-level planning. This opens a path for robotics systems to leverage actively maintained agent infrastructure and benefit directly from ongoing advances in frontier models. Code is available at https://github.com/robiemusketeer/faea-sim
tldr: This paper introduces FAEA, an LLM agent framework for robotic manipulation that achieves comparable performance to VLA models, without requiring task-specific demonstrations or fine-tuning, and suggests using it for autonomous exploration and training data augmentation.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了 FAEA，一个用于机器人操作的 LLM 代理框架，它在不需要特定任务演示或微调的情况下，实现了与 VLA 模型相当的性能，并建议将其用于自主探索和训练数据增强。
summary_zh:
摘要：
机器人操作越来越多地采用视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型，这些模型表现出色，但通常需要特定于任务的演示和微调，并且在领域迁移下泛化能力较差。我们研究了最初为软件工程开发的通用大型语言模型（LLM）代理框架，是否可以作为具身操作的替代控制范式。我们引入了FAEA（基于前沿代理的具身代理），它将LLM代理框架直接应用于具身操作而无需修改。利用与软件代理调试代码相同的迭代推理能力，FAEA使具身代理能够推理操作策略。我们使用未经修改的前沿代理，Claude Agent SDK，在LIBERO、ManiSkill3和MetaWorld基准测试中进行了评估。在具备特权环境状态访问权限的情况下，FAEA分别实现了84.9%、85.7%和96%的成功率。这种任务成功水平接近于使用每个任务少于100个演示训练的VLA模型，而无需演示或微调。通过一轮人工反馈作为可选优化，LIBERO上的性能提高到88.2%。这种无需演示的能力具有直接的实用价值：FAEA能够自主探索模拟中的新场景，并生成成功的轨迹，用于具身学习中的训练数据增强。我们的结果表明，通用代理足以胜任一类由深思熟虑的、任务级规划主导的操作任务。这为机器人系统开辟了一条道路，使其能够利用积极维护的代理基础设施，并直接受益于前沿模型的持续进步。代码可在https://github.com/robiemusketeer/faea-sim获取。

title: Tactile-Force Alignment in Vision-Language-Action Models for Force-aware Manipulation
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently emerged as powerful generalists for robotic manipulation. However, due to their predominant reliance on visual modalities, they fundamentally lack the physical intuition required for contact-rich tasks that require precise force regulation and physical reasoning. Existing attempts to incorporate vision-based tactile sensing into VLA models typically treat tactile inputs as auxiliary visual textures, thereby overlooking the underlying correlation between surface deformation and interaction dynamics. To bridge this gap, we propose a paradigm shift from tactile-vision alignment to tactile-force alignment. Here, we introduce TaF-VLA, a framework that explicitly grounds high-dimensional tactile observations in physical interaction forces. To facilitate this, we develop an automated tactile-force data acquisition device and curate the TaF-Dataset, comprising over 10 million synchronized tactile observations, 6-axis force/torque, and matrix force map. To align sequential tactile observations with interaction forces, the central component of our approach is the Tactile-Force Adapter (TaF-Adapter), a tactile sensor encoder that extracts discretized latent information for encoding tactile observations. This mechanism ensures that the learned representations capture history-dependent, noise-insensitive physical dynamics rather than static visual textures. Finally, we integrate this force-aligned encoder into a VLA backbone. Extensive real-world experiments demonstrate that TaF-VLA policy significantly outperforms state-of-the-art tactile-vision-aligned and vision-only baselines on contact-rich tasks, verifying its ability to achieve robust, force-aware manipulation through cross-modal physical reasoning.
tldr: The paper introduces TaF-VLA, a framework for force-aware robotic manipulation that bridges the gap between tactile sensing and physical interaction forces by aligning tactile observations with force data and integrating it into a VLA model, demonstrating improved performance on contact-rich tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了TaF-VLA，一个用于力感知的机器人操作框架，通过将触觉观察与力数据对齐并将其集成到VLA模型中，弥合了触觉传感和物理交互力之间的差距，并在接触丰富的任务中展示了更高的性能。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型最近作为机器人操作的强大通用模型涌现。然而，由于它们主要依赖视觉模态，因此从根本上缺乏需要精确力调节和物理推理的富接触任务所需的物理直觉。现有将基于视觉的触觉传感整合到VLA模型中的尝试通常将触觉输入视为辅助视觉纹理，从而忽略了表面形变和交互动力学之间的潜在关联。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了一种范式转变，即从触觉-视觉对齐到触觉-力对齐。在此，我们引入TaF-VLA，这是一个将高维触觉观察显式地与物理交互力关联的框架。为了实现这一点，我们开发了一种自动化触觉-力数据采集设备，并构建了TaF-数据集，其中包含超过1000万个同步的触觉观察、六轴力/力矩和矩阵力图。为了将连续的触觉观察与交互力对齐，我们方法的核心组件是触觉-力适配器（TaF-Adapter），这是一个触觉传感器编码器，用于提取离散化的潜在信息以编码触觉观察。这种机制确保了学习到的表征能够捕捉到历史依赖的、噪声不敏感的物理动力学，而非静态的视觉纹理。最后，我们将这种力对齐编码器集成到VLA骨干网络中。大量的真实世界实验表明，TaF-VLA策略在富接触任务上显著优于最先进的触觉-视觉对齐和纯视觉基线，验证了其通过跨模态物理推理实现稳健的、力感知的操作能力。

title: Shallow-π: Knowledge Distillation for Flow-based VLAs
summary: The growing demand for real-time robotic deployment necessitates fast and on-device inference for vision-language-action (VLA) models. Within the VLA literature, efficiency has been extensively studied at the token level, such as visual token pruning. In contrast, systematic transformer layer reduction has received limited attention and, to the best of our knowledge, has not been explored for flow-based VLA models under knowledge distillation. In this work, we propose Shallow-pi, a principled knowledge distillation framework that aggressively reduces the transformer depth of both the VLM backbone and the flow-based action head, compressing the model from 18 to 6 layers. Shallow-pi achieves over two times faster inference with less than one percent absolute drop in success rate on standard manipulation benchmarks, establishing state-of-the-art performance among reduced VLA models. Crucially, we validate our approach through industrial-scale real-world experiments on Jetson Orin and Jetson Thor across multiple robot platforms, including humanoid systems, in complex and dynamic manipulation scenarios.
tldr: The paper introduces Shallow-pi, a knowledge distillation framework for compressing flow-based VLA models, achieving faster inference speeds with minimal performance loss and tested on real-world robotic platforms.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 Shallow-pi，一个用于压缩基于流的 VLA 模型的知识蒸馏框架，在实际机器人平台上测试，实现了更快的推理速度，且性能损失最小。
summary_zh: 对于视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型，实时机器人部署的需求日益增长，这要求快速且在设备端进行推理。在VLA文献中，效率已在token层面得到了广泛研究，例如视觉token剪枝。相比之下，系统性的Transformer层缩减受到的关注有限，并且据我们所知，尚未在基于流的VLA模型中，通过知识蒸馏进行探索。在这项工作中，我们提出了Shallow-pi，一个有原则的知识蒸馏框架，可以大幅减少VLM骨干网络和基于流的动作头的Transformer深度，从而将模型从18层压缩到6层。Shallow-pi在标准操作基准测试上，以小于百分之一的绝对成功率下降，实现了超过两倍的推理速度，从而在精简VLA模型中建立了最先进的性能。至关重要的是，我们在Jetson Orin和Jetson Thor上，跨多个机器人平台（包括人形机器人系统）进行了工业规模的真实世界实验，验证了我们方法在复杂和动态操作场景中的有效性。

title: TouchGuide: Inference-Time Steering of Visuomotor Policies via Touch Guidance
summary: Fine-grained and contact-rich manipulation remain challenging for robots, largely due to the underutilization of tactile feedback. To address this, we introduce TouchGuide, a novel cross-policy visuo-tactile fusion paradigm that fuses modalities within a low-dimensional action space. Specifically, TouchGuide operates in two stages to guide a pre-trained diffusion or flow-matching visuomotor policy at inference time. First, the policy produces a coarse, visually-plausible action using only visual inputs during early sampling. Second, a task-specific Contact Physical Model (CPM) provides tactile guidance to steer and refine the action, ensuring it aligns with realistic physical contact conditions. Trained through contrastive learning on limited expert demonstrations, the CPM provides a tactile-informed feasibility score to steer the sampling process toward refined actions that satisfy physical contact constraints. Furthermore, to facilitate TouchGuide training with high-quality and cost-effective data, we introduce TacUMI, a data collection system. TacUMI achieves a favorable trade-off between precision and affordability; by leveraging rigid fingertips, it obtains direct tactile feedback, thereby enabling the collection of reliable tactile data. Extensive experiments on five challenging contact-rich tasks, such as shoe lacing and chip handover, show that TouchGuide consistently and significantly outperforms state-of-the-art visuo-tactile policies.
tldr: TouchGuide is a novel visuotactile fusion paradigm that leverages a contrastively trained Contact Physical Model (CPM) to refine visuomotor policy actions at inference time using tactile feedback, achieving SOTA performance in contact-rich manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: TouchGuide 是一种新颖的视觉触觉融合范例，它利用经过对比学习训练的接触物理模型 (CPM) 在推理时使用触觉反馈来改进视觉运动策略动作，从而在接触丰富的操作任务中实现 SOTA 性能。
summary_zh: 精细且接触丰富的操作对机器人来说仍然具有挑战性，这很大程度上是由于触觉反馈的利用不足。为了解决这个问题，我们提出TouchGuide，一种新型的跨策略视觉-触觉融合范式，它在一个低维动作空间内融合多种模态。具体而言，TouchGuide分两个阶段操作，在推理时引导一个预训练的扩散或流匹配视觉运动策略。首先，该策略在早期采样阶段仅使用视觉输入产生一个粗略的、视觉上可行的动作。其次，一个特定任务的接触物理模型（CPM）提供触觉引导来操控和细化该动作，确保其与实际的物理接触条件一致。通过在有限的专家演示上进行对比学习训练，CPM提供一个触觉信息化的可行性评分，以引导采样过程产生满足物理接触约束的精细化动作。此外，为了便于使用高质量且经济高效的数据进行TouchGuide训练，我们引入了TacUMI，一种数据收集系统。TacUMI在精度和经济性之间实现了良好的权衡；通过利用刚性指尖，它获得直接的触觉反馈，从而能够收集可靠的触觉数据。在五个具有挑战性的接触丰富任务（如鞋带系紧和芯片移交）上的大量实验表明，TouchGuide始终且显著地优于最先进的视觉-触觉策略。

title: Real-Time Robot Execution with Masked Action Chunking
summary: Real-time execution is essential for cyber-physical systems such as robots. These systems operate in dynamic real-world environments where even small delays can undermine responsiveness and compromise performance. Asynchronous inference has recently emerged as a system-level paradigm for real-time robot manipulation, enabling the next action chunk to be predicted while the current one is being executed. While this approach achieves real-time responsiveness, naive integration often results in execution failure. Previous methods attributed this failure to inter-chunk discontinuity and developed test-time algorithms to smooth chunk boundaries. In contrast, we identify another critical yet overlooked factor: intra-chunk inconsistency, where the robot's executed action chunk partially misaligns with its current perception. To address this, we propose REMAC, which learns corrective adjustments on the pretrained policy through masked action chunking, enabling the policy to remain resilient under mismatches between intended actions and actual execution during asynchronous inference. In addition, we introduce a prefix-preserved sampling procedure to reinforce inter-chunk continuity. Overall, our method delivers more reliable policies without incurring additional latency. Extensive experiments in both simulation and real-world settings demonstrate that our method enables faster task execution, maintains robustness across varying delays, and consistently achieves higher completion rates.
tldr: The paper introduces REMAC, a method that uses masked action chunking to improve the robustness and speed of real-time robot execution by addressing intra-chunk inconsistencies arising from asynchronous inference, demonstrated in both simulation and real-world scenarios.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了REMAC，一种通过掩码动作分块来提高实时机器人执行的鲁棒性和速度的方法，通过解决异步推理引起的块内不一致性，并在模拟和现实环境中进行了演示。
summary_zh: 对于诸如机器人之类的网络物理系统，实时执行至关重要。 这些系统在动态的现实环境中运行，即使是微小的延迟也可能损害响应能力并影响性能。 异步推理最近作为一种系统级范例出现在实时机器人操作领域，它允许在当前动作块执行的同时预测下一个动作块。 尽管这种方法实现了实时响应，但简单的集成通常会导致执行失败。 以往的方法将这种失败归因于块间不连续性，并开发了测试时算法来平滑块边界。 相反，我们指出了另一个关键但被忽视的因素：块内不一致性，即机器人执行的动作块与当前感知的部分错位。 为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 REMAC，它通过掩码动作分块来学习对预训练策略的校正调整，使该策略在异步推理期间，即使在预期动作与实际执行不匹配的情况下也能保持弹性。 此外，我们引入了一种前缀保留采样程序来加强块间的连续性。 总而言之，我们的方法提供了更可靠的策略，且不会产生额外的延迟。 在模拟和真实环境中的大量实验表明，我们的方法能够实现更快的任务执行，在不同的延迟下保持鲁棒性，并始终实现更高的完成率。

title: E2HiL: Entropy-Guided Sample Selection for Efficient Real-World Human-in-the-Loop Reinforcement Learning
summary: Human-in-the-loop guidance has emerged as an effective approach for enabling faster convergence in online reinforcement learning (RL) of complex real-world manipulation tasks. However, existing human-in-the-loop RL (HiL-RL) frameworks often suffer from low sample efficiency, requiring substantial human interventions to achieve convergence and thereby leading to high labor costs. To address this, we propose a sample-efficient real-world human-in-the-loop RL framework named \method, which requires fewer human intervention by actively selecting informative samples. Specifically, stable reduction of policy entropy enables improved trade-off between exploration and exploitation with higher sample efficiency. We first build influence functions of different samples on the policy entropy, which is efficiently estimated by the covariance of action probabilities and soft advantages of policies. Then we select samples with moderate values of influence functions, where shortcut samples that induce sharp entropy drops and noisy samples with negligible effect are pruned. Extensive experiments on four real-world manipulation tasks demonstrate that \method achieves a 42.1\% higher success rate while requiring 10.1\% fewer human interventions compared to the state-of-the-art HiL-RL method, validating its effectiveness. The project page providing code, videos, and mathematical formulations can be found at https://e2hil.github.io/.
tldr: The paper introduces E2HiL, a human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning framework that improves sample efficiency by actively selecting informative samples based on policy entropy, leading to fewer human interventions and higher success rates in real-world manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了E2HiL，一种人机协作强化学习框架，通过基于策略熵主动选择信息丰富的样本来提高样本效率，从而减少人为干预并在现实世界的操作任务中实现更高的成功率。
summary_zh: 人机循环指导已成为一种有效的方法，能够加速复杂现实世界操作任务在线强化学习 (RL) 的收敛。然而，现有的人机循环强化学习 (HiL-RL) 框架通常存在样本效率低的问题，需要大量的人工干预才能实现收敛，从而导致高昂的人力成本。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种样本高效的现实世界人机循环强化学习框架，名为 \method，它通过主动选择信息丰富的样本来减少人工干预。具体来说，策略熵的稳定降低使得探索和利用之间能够实现更好的权衡，从而提高样本效率。我们首先构建不同样本对策略熵的影响函数，该策略熵可以通过行动概率和策略软优势的协方差来有效地估计。然后，我们选择影响函数值适中的样本，从而剪除导致熵急剧下降的快捷样本和影响可忽略不计的噪声样本。在四个现实世界操作任务上的大量实验表明，与最先进的HiL-RL方法相比，\method实现了高出42.1%的成功率，同时需要减少10.1%的人工干预，验证了其有效性。包含代码、视频和数学公式的项目页面可以在 https://e2hil.github.io/ 找到。

title: Reinforcement Learning via Self-Distillation
summary: Large language models are increasingly post-trained with reinforcement learning in verifiable domains such as code and math. Yet, current methods for reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) learn only from a scalar outcome reward per attempt, creating a severe credit-assignment bottleneck. Many verifiable environments actually provide rich textual feedback, such as runtime errors or judge evaluations, that explain why an attempt failed. We formalize this setting as reinforcement learning with rich feedback and introduce Self-Distillation Policy Optimization (SDPO), which converts tokenized feedback into a dense learning signal without any external teacher or explicit reward model. SDPO treats the current model conditioned on feedback as a self-teacher and distills its feedback-informed next-token predictions back into the policy. In this way, SDPO leverages the model's ability to retrospectively identify its own mistakes in-context. Across scientific reasoning, tool use, and competitive programming on LiveCodeBench v6, SDPO improves sample efficiency and final accuracy over strong RLVR baselines. Notably, SDPO also outperforms baselines in standard RLVR environments that only return scalar feedback by using successful rollouts as implicit feedback for failed attempts. Finally, applying SDPO to individual questions at test time accelerates discovery on difficult binary-reward tasks, achieving the same discovery probability as best-of-k sampling or multi-turn conversations with 3x fewer attempts.
tldr: The paper introduces Self-Distillation Policy Optimization (SDPO), a reinforcement learning method that leverages rich textual feedback from verifiable environments by distilling a self-teacher policy, improving sample efficiency and accuracy in tasks like scientific reasoning and code generation.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了自蒸馏策略优化（SDPO），一种强化学习方法，通过提炼自教师策略来利用来自可验证环境的丰富文本反馈，从而提高科学推理和代码生成等任务的样本效率和准确性。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型越来越多地使用强化学习在可验证领域（如代码和数学）中进行后训练。然而，当前使用可验证奖励进行强化学习（RLVR）的方法仅从每次尝试的标量结果奖励中学习，从而造成严重的信用分配瓶颈。许多可验证环境实际上提供了丰富的文本反馈，例如运行时错误或评判评估，来解释尝试失败的原因。我们将此场景形式化为带有丰富反馈的强化学习，并引入了自蒸馏策略优化（SDPO），它将分词后的反馈转换为密集的学习信号，而无需任何外部教师或显式奖励模型。SDPO 将以反馈为条件的当前模型视为自教师，并将反馈指导下的下一步预测蒸馏回策略中。通过这种方式，SDPO 利用模型回顾性地识别其自身上下文中错误的能力。在科学推理、工具使用以及 LiveCodeBench v6 上的竞争性编程中，SDPO 提高了样本效率和最终准确性，优于强大的 RLVR 基线。值得注意的是，在仅返回标量反馈的标准 RLVR 环境中，SDPO 也胜过基线，它通过将成功 rollout 作为失败尝试的隐式反馈。最后，在测试时将 SDPO 应用于单个问题，加速了困难的二元奖励任务的探索，以比最佳 k 采样或多轮对话少 3 倍的尝试次数实现了相同的探索概率。

title: Non-Markov Multi-Round Conversational Image Generation with History-Conditioned MLLMs
summary: Conversational image generation requires a model to follow user instructions across multiple rounds of interaction, grounded in interleaved text and images that accumulate as chat history. While recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can generate and edit images, most existing multi-turn benchmarks and training recipes are effectively Markov: the next output depends primarily on the most recent image, enabling shortcut solutions that ignore long-range history. In this work we formalize and target the more challenging non-Markov setting, where a user may refer back to earlier states, undo changes, or reference entities introduced several rounds ago. We present (i) non-Markov multi-round data construction strategies, including rollback-style editing that forces retrieval of earlier visual states and name-based multi-round personalization that binds names to appearances across rounds; (ii) a history-conditioned training and inference framework with token-level caching to prevent multi-round identity drift; and (iii) enabling improvements for high-fidelity image reconstruction and editable personalization, including a reconstruction-based DiT detokenizer and a multi-stage fine-tuning curriculum. We demonstrate that explicitly training for non-Markov interactions yields substantial improvements in multi-round consistency and instruction compliance, while maintaining strong single-round editing and personalization.
tldr: The paper addresses the non-Markovian nature of multi-round conversational image generation by proposing data construction strategies, a history-conditioned training framework with token-level caching, and improvements for high-fidelity image reconstruction and editable personalization. Explicitly training for non-Markov interactions yields significant improvements in multi-round consistency and instruction compliance.
tldr_zh: 本文致力于解决多轮对话图像生成中的非马尔可夫性质，提出了数据构建策略、带有token-level缓存的历史条件训练框架，以及对高保真图像重建和可编辑个性化的改进。针对非马尔可夫交互的显式训练显著提高了多轮一致性和指令遵循。
summary_zh: 对话式图像生成要求模型在多轮交互中遵循用户指令，并以交错的文本和图像作为基础，这些文本和图像会随着聊天历史的积累而不断增加。虽然最近的多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）可以生成和编辑图像，但大多数现有的多轮基准和训练方法实际上是马尔可夫的：下一个输出主要取决于最近的一张图像，这使得模型能够使用忽略长期历史的捷径解决方案。在这项工作中，我们形式化并针对更具挑战性的非马尔可夫设置，在这种设置下，用户可能会回顾之前的状态，撤销更改，或引用几轮之前引入的实体。我们提出了：（i）非马尔可夫多轮数据构建策略，包括强制检索早期视觉状态的回滚式编辑，以及跨多轮将名称与外观绑定的基于名称的多轮个性化；（ii）一种带有token级缓存的历史条件训练和推理框架，以防止多轮身份漂移；以及（iii）支持高保真图像重建和可编辑个性化的改进方法，包括基于重建的DiT反标记器和多阶段微调课程。我们证明，针对非马尔可夫交互的显式训练能够在多轮一致性和指令遵循方面产生显著改进，同时保持强大的单轮编辑和个性化能力。

title: Learning Contextual Runtime Monitors for Safe AI-Based Autonomy
summary: We introduce a novel framework for learning context-aware runtime monitors for AI-based control ensembles. Machine-learning (ML) controllers are increasingly deployed in (autonomous) cyber-physical systems because of their ability to solve complex decision-making tasks. However, their accuracy can degrade sharply in unfamiliar environments, creating significant safety concerns. Traditional ensemble methods aim to improve robustness by averaging or voting across multiple controllers, yet this often dilutes the specialized strengths that individual controllers exhibit in different operating contexts. We argue that, rather than blending controller outputs, a monitoring framework should identify and exploit these contextual strengths. In this paper, we reformulate the design of safe AI-based control ensembles as a contextual monitoring problem. A monitor continuously observes the system's context and selects the controller best suited to the current conditions. To achieve this, we cast monitor learning as a contextual learning task and draw on techniques from contextual multi-armed bandits. Our approach comes with two key benefits: (1) theoretical safety guarantees during controller selection, and (2) improved utilization of controller diversity. We validate our framework in two simulated autonomous driving scenarios, demonstrating significant improvements in both safety and performance compared to non-contextual baselines.
tldr: This paper proposes a framework for learning context-aware runtime monitors that select the best AI controller for autonomous systems based on the current context, improving safety and performance with theoretical guarantees.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种学习上下文感知运行时监控器的框架，该监控器根据当前上下文选择最适合自主系统的AI控制器，从而提高安全性和性能，并具有理论保证。
summary_zh: 我们提出了一种用于学习基于人工智能的控制集成系统的上下文感知运行时监控器的新框架。 机器学习 (ML) 控制器因其解决复杂决策任务的能力而越来越多地部署在（自主）信息物理系统中。 然而，它们的准确性在不熟悉的环境中会急剧下降，从而产生重大的安全问题。 传统的集成方法旨在通过对多个控制器进行平均或投票来提高鲁棒性，但这通常会削弱各个控制器在不同操作环境中表现出的特殊优势。 我们认为，监控框架应该识别并利用这些情境优势，而不是混合控制器输出。 在本文中，我们将基于人工智能的安全控制集成系统的设计重新定义为一个上下文监控问题。 监控器持续观察系统上下文，并选择最适合当前条件的控制器。 为了实现这一点，我们将监控器学习建模为一个上下文学习任务，并借鉴上下文多臂老虎机技术。 我们的方法具有两个关键优势：（1）控制器选择期间的理论安全保证，以及（2）改进的控制器多样性利用率。 我们在两个模拟的自动驾驶场景中验证了我们的框架，与非上下文基线相比，在安全性和性能方面都得到了显著提升。

title: Investigating the Development of Task-Oriented Communication in Vision-Language Models
summary: We investigate whether \emph{LLM-based agents} can develop task-oriented communication protocols that differ from standard natural language in collaborative reasoning tasks. Our focus is on two core properties such task-oriented protocols may exhibit: Efficiency -- conveying task-relevant information more concisely than natural language, and Covertness -- becoming difficult for external observers to interpret, raising concerns about transparency and control. To investigate these aspects, we use a referential-game framework in which vision-language model (VLM) agents communicate, providing a controlled, measurable setting for evaluating language variants. Experiments show that VLMs can develop effective, task-adapted communication patterns. At the same time, they can develop covert protocols that are difficult for humans and external agents to interpret. We also observe spontaneous coordination between similar models without explicitly shared protocols. These findings highlight both the potential and the risks of task-oriented communication, and position referential games as a valuable testbed for future work in this area.
tldr: This paper explores whether vision-language models can develop efficient and covert communication protocols in collaborative reasoning tasks using a referential game framework, finding that they can indeed develop task-adapted communication and covert strategies.
tldr_zh: 该论文探讨了视觉语言模型是否可以在协作推理任务中使用指称游戏框架开发高效且隐蔽的通信协议，发现它们确实可以开发出适应任务的通信和隐蔽策略。
summary_zh: 我们研究了基于大型语言模型（LLM）的智能体是否能够开发出在协作推理任务中与标准自然语言不同的、面向任务的通信协议。我们的重点是此类面向任务的协议可能展现出的两个核心属性：效率——比自然语言更简洁地传达与任务相关的信息，以及隐蔽性——变得难以让外部观察者解读，从而引发对透明度和控制的担忧。为了研究这些方面，我们使用了一个指示参照博弈框架，在该框架中，视觉-语言模型（VLM）智能体进行通信，从而提供了一个可控、可测量的环境来评估语言变体。实验表明，视觉-语言模型可以开发出有效的、适应任务的通信模式。同时，它们也可以开发出人类和外部智能体难以解读的隐蔽协议。我们还观察到相似模型之间在没有明确共享协议的情况下自发协调。这些发现凸显了面向任务的通信的潜力和风险，并将指示参照博弈定位为该领域未来工作的一个有价值的试验平台。

title: Harder Is Better: Boosting Mathematical Reasoning via Difficulty-Aware GRPO and Multi-Aspect Question Reformulation
summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) offers a robust mechanism for enhancing mathematical reasoning in large models. However, we identify a systematic lack of emphasis on more challenging questions in existing methods from both algorithmic and data perspectives, despite their importance for refining underdeveloped capabilities. Algorithmically, widely used Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) suffers from an implicit imbalance where the magnitude of policy updates is lower for harder questions. Data-wise, augmentation approaches primarily rephrase questions to enhance diversity without systematically increasing intrinsic difficulty. To address these issues, we propose a two-dual MathForge framework to improve mathematical reasoning by targeting harder questions from both perspectives, which comprises a Difficulty-Aware Group Policy Optimization (DGPO) algorithm and a Multi-Aspect Question Reformulation (MQR) strategy. Specifically, DGPO first rectifies the implicit imbalance in GRPO via difficulty-balanced group advantage estimation, and further prioritizes harder questions by difficulty-aware question-level weighting. Meanwhile, MQR reformulates questions across multiple aspects to increase difficulty while maintaining the original gold answer. Overall, MathForge forms a synergistic loop: MQR expands the data frontier, and DGPO effectively learns from the augmented data. Extensive experiments show that MathForge significantly outperforms existing methods on various mathematical reasoning tasks. The code and augmented data are all available at https://github.com/AMAP-ML/MathForge.
tldr: This paper introduces MathForge, a framework using difficulty-aware reinforcement learning and multi-aspect question reformulation to improve mathematical reasoning in large models, demonstrating significant performance gains.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MathForge，一个利用难度感知的强化学习和多方面问题重构的框架，旨在提高大型模型中的数学推理能力，实验结果表明该框架具有显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）为提升大型模型中的数学推理能力提供了一个强大的机制。然而，尽管更具挑战性的问题对于完善未充分发展的能力至关重要，但我们发现，从算法和数据角度来看，现有方法系统性地缺乏对这些问题的重视。在算法方面，广泛使用的群体相对策略优化（GRPO）存在一个隐式的不平衡，即对于更难的问题，策略更新的幅度较低。在数据方面，增强方法主要通过改述问题来增强多样性，而没有系统地增加内在难度。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一个双重MathForge框架，旨在从算法和数据两个角度入手，通过针对更难的问题来提高数学推理能力，该框架包含一个难度感知的群体策略优化（DGPO）算法和一个多方面的问题重构（MQR）策略。具体而言，DGPO首先通过难度平衡的群体优势估计来纠正GRPO中的隐式不平衡，并进一步通过难度感知的问题级别加权来优先考虑更难的问题。同时，MQR从多个方面重构问题以增加难度，同时保持原始的黄金答案。总而言之，MathForge形成了一个协同循环：MQR扩展了数据边界，而DGPO则有效地从增强的数据中学习。大量实验表明，MathForge在各种数学推理任务上显著优于现有方法。代码和增强数据均可在https://github.com/AMAP-ML/MathForge上获取。

title: PathWise: Planning through World Model for Automated Heuristic Design via Self-Evolving LLMs
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have enabled automated heuristic design (AHD) for combinatorial optimization problems (COPs), but existing frameworks' reliance on fixed evolutionary rules and static prompt templates often leads to myopic heuristic generation, redundant evaluations, and limited reasoning about how new heuristics should be derived. We propose a novel multi-agent reasoning framework, referred to as Planning through World Model for Automated Heuristic Design via Self-Evolving LLMs (PathWise), which formulates heuristic generation as a sequential decision process over an entailment graph serving as a compact, stateful memory of the search trajectory. This approach allows the system to carry forward past decisions and reuse or avoid derivation information across generations. A policy agent plans evolutionary actions, a world model agent generates heuristic rollouts conditioned on those actions, and critic agents provide routed reflections summarizing lessons from prior steps, shifting LLM-based AHD from trial-and-error evolution toward state-aware planning through reasoning. Experiments across diverse COPs show that PathWise converges faster to better heuristics, generalizes across different LLM backbones, and scales to larger problem sizes.
tldr: The paper introduces PathWise, a novel framework that uses self-evolving LLMs and a world model to plan heuristic generation for combinatorial optimization problems, resulting in faster convergence, better heuristics, and improved generalization.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为PathWise的新框架，该框架利用自我演化的LLM和世界模型来规划组合优化问题的启发式生成，从而实现更快的收敛、更好的启发式方法和改进的泛化。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）已经实现了针对组合优化问题（COPs）的自动化启发式设计（AHD），但现有框架对固定进化规则和静态提示模板的依赖通常导致短视的启发式生成、冗余的评估以及对如何推导新启发式的有限推理。我们提出了一种新颖的多智能体推理框架，称为“通过世界模型进行规划，以利用自进化LLMs进行自动化启发式设计”（PathWise），该框架将启发式生成表述为一个在蕴涵图上的序列决策过程，该蕴涵图充当搜索轨迹的紧凑、有状态的记忆。这种方法允许系统延续过去的决策，并在不同生成中重用或避免推导信息。策略智能体规划进化动作，世界模型智能体根据这些动作生成启发式展开，而批评智能体则提供路由反射，总结先前步骤的经验教训，从而将基于LLM的AHD从试错进化转变为通过推理进行的状态感知规划。在各种COPs上的实验表明，PathWise能够更快地收敛到更好的启发式方法，泛化到不同的LLM骨干网络，并扩展到更大的问题规模。

title: Policy of Thoughts: Scaling LLM Reasoning via Test-time Policy Evolution
summary: Large language models (LLMs) struggle with complex, long-horizon reasoning due to instability caused by their frozen policy assumption. Current test-time scaling methods treat execution feedback merely as an external signal for filtering or rewriting trajectories, without internalizing it to improve the underlying reasoning strategy. Inspired by Popper's epistemology of "conjectures and refutations," we argue that intelligence requires real-time evolution of the model's policy through learning from failed attempts. We introduce Policy of Thoughts (PoT), a framework that recasts reasoning as a within-instance online optimization process. PoT first generates diverse candidate solutions via an efficient exploration mechanism, then uses Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to update a transient LoRA adapter based on execution feedback. This closed-loop design enables dynamic, instance-specific refinement of the model's reasoning priors. Experiments show that PoT dramatically boosts performance: a 4B model achieves 49.71% accuracy on LiveCodeBench, outperforming GPT-4o and DeepSeek-V3 despite being over 50 smaller.
tldr: The paper introduces Policy of Thoughts (PoT), a framework that improves LLM reasoning by dynamically updating the model's policy during test-time using execution feedback and Group Relative Policy Optimization, achieving strong performance on LiveCodeBench.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Policy of Thoughts (PoT)，一个通过在测试期间使用执行反馈和组相对策略优化动态更新模型策略来改进LLM推理的框架，并在LiveCodeBench上取得了优异的性能。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）由于其固定的策略假设所导致的不稳定性，难以处理复杂的、长程推理任务。目前的测试时扩展方法仅仅将执行反馈视为外部信号，用于过滤或重写轨迹，而没有将其内化以改进底层的推理策略。受到波普尔“猜想与反驳”认识论的启发，我们认为智能需要通过从失败尝试中学习来实时演化模型的策略。我们引入了思维策略（Policy of Thoughts, PoT）框架，该框架将推理重塑为实例内的在线优化过程。PoT首先通过高效的探索机制生成多样化的候选解决方案，然后使用群体相对策略优化（Group Relative Policy Optimization, GRPO）基于执行反馈来更新一个瞬态的LoRA适配器。这种闭环设计实现了模型推理先验的动态、实例特定的优化。实验表明，PoT极大地提高了性能：一个4B模型在LiveCodeBench上实现了49.71%的准确率，超越了GPT-4o和DeepSeek-V3，尽管模型规模小50倍以上。

title: MobileBench-OL: A Comprehensive Chinese Benchmark for Evaluating Mobile GUI Agents in Real-World Environment
summary: Recent advances in mobile Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents highlight the growing need for comprehensive evaluation benchmarks. While new online benchmarks offer more realistic testing than offline ones, they tend to focus on the agents' task instruction-following ability while neglecting their reasoning and exploration ability. Moreover, these benchmarks do not consider the random noise in real-world mobile environments. This leads to a gap between benchmarks and real-world environments. To addressing these limitations, we propose MobileBench-OL, an online benchmark with 1080 tasks from 80 Chinese apps. It measures task execution, complex reasoning, and noise robustness of agents by including 5 subsets, which set multiple evaluation dimensions. We also provide an auto-eval framework with a reset mechanism, enabling stable and repeatable real-world benchmarking. Evaluating 12 leading GUI agents on MobileBench-OL shows significant room for improvement to meet real-world requirements. Human evaluation further confirms that MobileBench-OL can reliably measure the performance of leading GUI agents in real environments. Our data and code will be released upon acceptance.
tldr: The paper introduces MobileBench-OL, a new online Chinese benchmark for evaluating mobile GUI agents, focusing on task execution, reasoning, and noise robustness in real-world environments. It addresses the limitations of existing benchmarks by incorporating real-world noise and complex reasoning tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个新的在线中文基准测试 MobileBench-OL，用于评估移动 GUI 代理，重点关注真实环境中任务执行、推理和噪声鲁棒性。它通过包含真实世界的噪声和复杂的推理任务来解决现有基准测试的局限性。
summary_zh: 移动图形用户界面（GUI）代理的最新进展凸显了对全面评估基准日益增长的需求。 虽然新的在线基准比离线基准提供更真实的测试，但它们往往侧重于代理的任务指令遵循能力，而忽略了它们的推理和探索能力。 此外，这些基准没有考虑到真实世界移动环境中的随机噪声。 这导致基准与真实世界环境之间存在差距。 为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了MobileBench-OL，一个包含来自80个中国App的1080个任务的在线基准。 它通过包含5个子集来衡量代理的任务执行、复杂推理和噪声鲁棒性，从而设置了多个评估维度。 我们还提供了一个带有重置机制的自动评估框架，从而实现稳定和可重复的真实世界基准测试。 在MobileBench-OL上评估12个领先的GUI代理表明，要满足真实世界的要求，还有很大的改进空间。 人工评估进一步证实，MobileBench-OL可以可靠地衡量领先的GUI代理在真实环境中的性能。 我们的数据和代码将在接受后发布。

title: Endogenous Reprompting: Self-Evolving Cognitive Alignment for Unified Multimodal Models
summary: Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) exhibit strong understanding, yet this capability often fails to effectively guide generation. We identify this as a Cognitive Gap: the model lacks the understanding of how to enhance its own generation process. To bridge this gap, we propose Endogenous Reprompting, a mechanism that transforms the model's understanding from a passive encoding process into an explicit generative reasoning step by generating self-aligned descriptors during generation. To achieve this, we introduce SEER (Self-Evolving Evaluator and Reprompter), a training framework that establishes a two-stage endogenous loop using only 300 samples from a compact proxy task, Visual Instruction Elaboration. First, Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) activates the model's latent evaluation ability via curriculum learning, producing a high-fidelity endogenous reward signal. Second, Reinforcement Learning with Model-rewarded Thinking (RLMT) leverages this signal to optimize the generative reasoning policy. Experiments show that SEER consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in evaluation accuracy, reprompting efficiency, and generation quality, without sacrificing general multimodal capabilities.
tldr: The paper introduces Endogenous Reprompting, a method using Reinforcement Learning to improve the generation quality of Unified Multimodal Models by enabling them to self-generate and evaluate prompts during the generation process, closing the 'Cognitive Gap'.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了内生重提示（Endogenous Reprompting），一种使用强化学习的方法，通过使统一多模态模型能够在生成过程中自我生成和评估提示，从而提高其生成质量，弥合了“认知差距”。
summary_zh: 统一多模态模型(UMMs)展现出强大的理解能力，然而这种能力往往无法有效地指导生成。我们将其识别为认知鸿沟：模型缺乏增强自身生成过程的理解。为了弥合这一鸿沟，我们提出了内生重提示，这是一种机制，通过在生成过程中生成自对齐的描述符，将模型的理解从被动的编码过程转变为显式的生成推理步骤。为了实现这一点，我们引入了SEER（自进化评估器和重提示器），这是一个训练框架，仅使用来自紧凑代理任务——视觉指令细化的300个样本，建立一个两阶段的内生循环。首先，具有可验证奖励的强化学习(RLVR)通过课程学习激活模型的潜在评估能力，产生高保真度的内生奖励信号。其次，具有模型奖励思维的强化学习(RLMT)利用该信号来优化生成推理策略。实验表明，SEER在评估准确率、重提示效率和生成质量方面始终优于最先进的基线，且不牺牲一般多模态能力。

title: Meta-Cognitive Reinforcement Learning with Self-Doubt and Recovery
summary:
Robust reinforcement learning methods typically focus on suppressing unreliable experiences or corrupted rewards, but they lack the ability to reason about the reliability of their own learning process. As a result, such methods often either overreact to noise by becoming overly conservative or fail catastrophically when uncertainty accumulates.
  In this work, we propose a meta-cognitive reinforcement learning framework that enables an agent to assess, regulate, and recover its learning behavior based on internally estimated reliability signals. The proposed method introduces a meta-trust variable driven by Value Prediction Error Stability (VPES), which modulates learning dynamics via fail-safe regulation and gradual trust recovery.
  Experiments on continuous-control benchmarks with reward corruption demonstrate that recovery-enabled meta-cognitive control achieves higher average returns and significantly reduces late-stage training failures compared to strong robustness baselines.
tldr: This paper proposes a meta-cognitive reinforcement learning framework with a meta-trust variable driven by value prediction error stability, enabling agents to assess, regulate, and recover their learning behavior in noisy environments, demonstrating improved performance on continuous control benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一个元认知强化学习框架，其中包含一个由价值预测误差稳定性驱动的元信任变量，使智能体能够在噪声环境中评估、调节和恢复其学习行为，并在连续控制基准测试中表现出改进的性能。
summary_zh:
稳健强化学习方法通常侧重于抑制不可靠经验或受损奖励，但缺乏对自身学习过程可靠性进行推理的能力。因此，这些方法通常要么对噪声反应过度而变得过于保守，要么在不确定性累积时发生灾难性故障。

在本文中，我们提出了一个元认知强化学习框架，该框架使智能体能够根据内部估计的可靠性信号来评估、调节和恢复其学习行为。该方法引入了一个由价值预测误差稳定性（VPES）驱动的元信任变量，该变量通过故障安全调节和逐步信任恢复来调制学习动态。

在具有奖励腐败的连续控制基准测试上的实验表明，与强大的稳健性基线相比，具有恢复功能的元认知控制获得了更高的平均回报，并显著减少了后期训练的失败。

title: Benchmarking Reward Hack Detection in Code Environments via Contrastive Analysis
summary: Recent advances in reinforcement learning for code generation have made robust environments essential to prevent reward hacking. As LLMs increasingly serve as evaluators in code-based RL, their ability to detect reward hacking remains understudied. In this paper, we propose a novel taxonomy of reward exploits spanning across 54 categories and introduce TRACE (Testing Reward Anomalies in Code Environments), a synthetically curated and human-verified benchmark containing 517 testing trajectories. Unlike prior work that evaluates reward hack detection in isolated classification scenarios, we contrast these evaluations with a more realistic, contrastive anomaly detection setup on TRACE. Our experiments reveal that models capture reward hacks more effectively in contrastive settings than in isolated classification settings, with GPT-5.2 with highest reasoning mode achieving the best detection rate at 63%, up from 45% in isolated settings on TRACE. Building on this insight, we demonstrate that state-of-the-art models struggle significantly more with semantically contextualized reward hacks compared to syntactically contextualized ones. We further conduct qualitative analyses of model behaviors, as well as ablation studies showing that the ratio of benign to hacked trajectories and analysis cluster sizes substantially impact detection performance. We release the benchmark and evaluation harness to enable the community to expand TRACE and evaluate their models.
tldr: The paper introduces TRACE, a benchmark for detecting reward hacking in code environments using LLMs as evaluators, and finds that contrastive analysis improves detection rates. It highlights challenges with semantically contextualized reward hacks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了TRACE，一个用于检测代码环境中奖励篡改的基准测试，使用LLM作为评估者，并发现对比分析提高了检测率。它强调了在语义上下文中奖励篡改的挑战。
summary_zh: 代码生成强化学习的最新进展使稳健的环境对于防止奖励利用至关重要。随着大型语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地在基于代码的强化学习中充当评估器，它们检测奖励利用的能力仍未得到充分研究。在本文中，我们提出一种新型奖励利用分类法，涵盖 54 个类别，并引入 TRACE（代码环境中的奖励异常测试），这是一个经过人工验证的综合基准，包含 517 条测试轨迹。与先前在孤立分类场景中评估奖励利用检测的工作不同，我们在 TRACE 上将这些评估与更真实的对比异常检测设置进行对比。我们的实验表明，模型在对比设置中比在孤立分类设置中更有效地捕获奖励利用，其中具有最高推理模式的 GPT-5.2 达到了最佳的检测率，为 63%，而孤立设置在 TRACE 上的检测率为 45%。基于这一洞见，我们表明，与句法上下文中的奖励利用相比，最先进的模型在语义上下文中的奖励利用方面表现更加困难。我们进一步对模型行为进行定性分析，并通过消融研究表明，良性轨迹与被利用轨迹的比率以及分析集群的大小会显著影响检测性能。我们发布该基准和评估框架，以使社区能够扩展 TRACE 并评估他们的模型。

title: Should I Have Expressed a Different Intent? Counterfactual Generation for LLM-Based Autonomous Control
summary: Large language model (LLM)-powered agents can translate high-level user intents into plans and actions in an environment. Yet after observing an outcome, users may wonder: What if I had phrased my intent differently? We introduce a framework that enables such counterfactual reasoning in agentic LLM-driven control scenarios, while providing formal reliability guarantees. Our approach models the closed-loop interaction between a user, an LLM-based agent, and an environment as a structural causal model (SCM), and leverages test-time scaling to generate multiple candidate counterfactual outcomes via probabilistic abduction. Through an offline calibration phase, the proposed conformal counterfactual generation (CCG) yields sets of counterfactual outcomes that are guaranteed to contain the true counterfactual outcome with high probability. We showcase the performance of CCG on a wireless network control use case, demonstrating significant advantages compared to naive re-execution baselines.
tldr: This paper introduces a framework for generating counterfactual outcomes in LLM-driven autonomous control, allowing users to explore 'what if' scenarios by phrasing their intents differently, with formal reliability guarantees using structural causal models.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种在基于LLM的自主控制中生成反事实结果的框架，使用户能够通过不同措辞的意图来探索“如果...会怎样”的场景，并使用结构因果模型提供正式的可靠性保证。
summary_zh: 基于大型语言模型 (LLM) 的智能体能够将高层次用户意图转化为环境中的计划和行动。然而，在观察到结果后，用户可能会想：如果我以不同的方式表达我的意图会怎样？我们引入一个框架，该框架能够在智能体 LLM 驱动的控制场景中实现此类反事实推理，同时提供正式的可靠性保证。我们的方法将用户、基于 LLM 的智能体和环境之间的闭环交互建模为一个结构因果模型 (SCM)，并利用测试时缩放通过概率性溯因生成多个候选反事实结果。通过离线校准阶段，所提出的保序反事实生成 (CCG) 产生一组反事实结果，这些结果保证以高概率包含真实的反事实结果。我们在无线网络控制用例中展示了 CCG 的性能，证明其相比于朴素的重新执行基线方法具有显著优势。

title: Compression Tells Intelligence: Visual Coding, Visual Token Technology, and the Unification
summary: "Compression Tells Intelligence", is supported by research in artificial intelligence, particularly concerning (multimodal) large language models (LLMs/MLLMs), where compression efficiency often correlates with improved model performance and capabilities. For compression, classical visual coding based on traditional information theory has developed over decades, achieving great success with numerous international industrial standards widely applied in multimedia (e.g., image/video) systems. Except that, the recent emergingvisual token technology of generative multi-modal large models also shares a similar fundamental objective like visual coding: maximizing semantic information fidelity during the representation learning while minimizing computational cost. Therefore, this paper provides a comprehensive overview of two dominant technique families first -- Visual Coding and Vision Token Technology -- then we further unify them from the aspect of optimization, discussing the essence of compression efficiency and model performance trade-off behind. Next, based on the proposed unified formulation bridging visual coding andvisual token technology, we synthesize bidirectional insights of themselves and forecast the next-gen visual codec and token techniques. Last but not least, we experimentally show a large potential of the task-oriented token developments in the more practical tasks like multimodal LLMs (MLLMs), AI-generated content (AIGC), and embodied AI, as well as shedding light on the future possibility of standardizing a general token technology like the traditional codecs (e.g., H.264/265) with high efficiency for a wide range of intelligent tasks in a unified and effective manner.
tldr: This paper unifies visual coding and visual token technology used in multimodal LLMs, suggesting bidirectional insights and future token standardization for improved efficiency across various AI tasks like MLLMs, AIGC, and embodied AI.
tldr_zh: 本文统一了多模态LLM中使用的视觉编码和视觉token技术，提出了双向见解和未来token标准化，以提高MLLM、AIGC和具身AI等各种AI任务的效率。
summary_zh: “压缩体现智能”这一观点得到了人工智能领域研究的支持，尤其是在（多模态）大型语言模型（LLMs/MLLMs）方面，压缩效率通常与模型性能和能力的提升相关。 在压缩方面，基于传统信息论的经典视觉编码经过数十年的发展，已取得巨大成功，并形成了众多广泛应用于多媒体（如图像/视频）系统的国际工业标准。 除此之外，最近涌现的生成式多模态大型模型的视觉 token 技术也与视觉编码有着相似的根本目标：在表示学习过程中最大化语义信息保真度，同时最小化计算成本。 因此，本文首先全面概述了两个主要的技术体系——视觉编码和视觉 token 技术——然后我们从优化的角度进一步统一它们，讨论了其背后压缩效率和模型性能之间的权衡本质。 接下来，基于提出的连接视觉编码和视觉 token 技术的统一公式，我们综合了它们之间的双向见解，并预测了下一代视觉编解码器和 token 技术。 最后，我们实验性地展示了面向任务的 token 技术在更实际的任务中，如多模态 LLM（MLLMs）、人工智能生成内容（AIGC）和具身 AI 中的巨大潜力，并阐明了未来可能以统一且有效的方式标准化通用 token 技术，使其像传统编解码器（如 H.264/265）一样，为广泛的智能任务提供高效率的可能性。

title: Hallucination Begins Where Saliency Drops
summary: Recent studies have examined attention dynamics in large vision-language models (LVLMs) to detect hallucinations. However, existing approaches remain limited in reliably distinguishing hallucinated from factually grounded outputs, as they rely solely on forward-pass attention patterns and neglect gradient-based signals that reveal how token influence propagates through the network. To bridge this gap, we introduce LVLMs-Saliency, a gradient-aware diagnostic framework that quantifies the visual grounding strength of each output token by fusing attention weights with their input gradients. Our analysis uncovers a decisive pattern: hallucinations frequently arise when preceding output tokens exhibit low saliency toward the prediction of the next token, signaling a breakdown in contextual memory retention. Leveraging this insight, we propose a dual-mechanism inference-time framework to mitigate hallucinations: (1) Saliency-Guided Rejection Sampling (SGRS), which dynamically filters candidate tokens during autoregressive decoding by rejecting those whose saliency falls below a context-adaptive threshold, thereby preventing coherence-breaking tokens from entering the output sequence; and (2) Local Coherence Reinforcement (LocoRE), a lightweight, plug-and-play module that strengthens attention from the current token to its most recent predecessors, actively counteracting the contextual forgetting behavior identified by LVLMs-Saliency. Extensive experiments across multiple LVLMs demonstrate that our method significantly reduces hallucination rates while preserving fluency and task performance, offering a robust and interpretable solution for enhancing model reliability. Code is available at: https://github.com/zhangbaijin/LVLMs-Saliency
tldr: This paper introduces a gradient-aware framework, LVLMs-Saliency, to detect and mitigate hallucinations in LVLMs by analyzing attention and gradient patterns, leading to improved model reliability through Saliency-Guided Rejection Sampling and Local Coherence Reinforcement.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一个名为LVLMs-Saliency的梯度感知框架，通过分析注意力和梯度模式来检测和减轻LVLMs中的幻觉，并通过Saliency-Guided Rejection Sampling和Local Coherence Reinforcement提高了模型的可靠性。
summary_zh: 近期研究已经对大型视觉-语言模型(LVLMs)中的注意力动态进行了研究，以检测幻觉。然而，现有的方法在可靠地区分幻觉输出和事实依据充分的输出方面仍然存在局限性，因为它们仅仅依赖于前向传递的注意力模式，而忽略了基于梯度的信号，这些信号揭示了Token影响如何在网络中传播。为了弥合这一差距，我们引入了LVLMs-Saliency，这是一个梯度感知的诊断框架，通过将注意力权重与其输入梯度融合，量化每个输出Token的视觉基础强度。我们的分析揭示了一种决定性的模式：当先前的输出Token对下一个Token的预测表现出低显著性时，幻觉经常出现，这表明上下文记忆保留的崩溃。利用这一洞察力，我们提出了一个双机制的推理时框架来缓解幻觉：（1）显著性引导的拒绝采样(SGRS)，它通过拒绝那些显著性低于上下文自适应阈值的候选Token，在自回归解码过程中动态地过滤候选Token，从而防止破坏一致性的Token进入输出序列；以及（2）局部一致性强化(LocoRE)，这是一个轻量级的、即插即用的模块，它加强了当前Token到其最近前任Token的注意力，积极地抵消了LVLMs-Saliency识别出的上下文遗忘行为。在多个LVLM上的大量实验表明，我们的方法显著降低了幻觉率，同时保持了流畅性和任务性能，为提高模型可靠性提供了一个稳健且可解释的解决方案。代码可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/zhangbaijin/LVLMs-Saliency

title: Sparse CLIP: Co-Optimizing Interpretability and Performance in Contrastive Learning
summary:
Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) has become a cornerstone in vision-language representation learning, powering diverse downstream tasks and serving as the default vision backbone in multimodal large language models (MLLMs). Despite its success, CLIP's dense and opaque latent representations pose significant interpretability challenges. A common assumption is that interpretability and performance are in tension: enforcing sparsity during training degrades accuracy, motivating recent post-hoc approaches such as Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs). However, these post-hoc approaches often suffer from degraded downstream performance and loss of CLIP's inherent multimodal capabilities, with most learned features remaining unimodal.
  We propose a simple yet effective approach that integrates sparsity directly into CLIP training, yielding representations that are both interpretable and performant. Compared to SAEs, our Sparse CLIP representations preserve strong downstream task performance, achieve superior interpretability, and retain multimodal capabilities. We show that multimodal sparse features enable straightforward semantic concept alignment and reveal training dynamics of how cross-modal knowledge emerges. Finally, as a proof of concept, we train a vision-language model on sparse CLIP representations that enables interpretable, vision-based steering capabilities. Our findings challenge conventional wisdom that interpretability requires sacrificing accuracy and demonstrate that interpretability and performance can be co-optimized, offering a promising design principle for future models.
tldr: This paper introduces Sparse CLIP, a method for training CLIP models with sparse latent representations to improve interpretability without sacrificing performance. It demonstrates improved interpretability, downstream performance, and multimodal capabilities compared to post-hoc sparsity methods.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了Sparse CLIP，一种使用稀疏潜在表示训练CLIP模型的方法，可在不牺牲性能的情况下提高可解释性。它展示了与事后稀疏方法相比，改进的可解释性、下游性能和多模态能力。
summary_zh:
对比语言-图像预训练（CLIP）已成为视觉-语言表征学习的基石，为各种下游任务提供动力，并充当多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）中的默认视觉骨干。尽管取得了成功，但CLIP的密集且不透明的潜在表征带来了显著的可解释性挑战。一个常见的假设是，可解释性和性能之间存在张力：在训练期间强制稀疏性会降低准确性，从而激发了最近的后处理方法，例如稀疏自编码器（SAE）。然而，这些后处理方法通常会遭受下游性能下降和CLIP固有跨模态能力丧失的困扰，并且大多数学习到的特征仍然是单模态的。

我们提出了一种简单而有效的方法，将稀疏性直接集成到CLIP训练中，从而产生既可解释又具有高性能的表征。与SAE相比，我们的稀疏CLIP表征保留了强大的下游任务性能，实现了卓越的可解释性，并保留了跨模态能力。我们表明，多模态稀疏特征能够实现直接的语义概念对齐，并揭示跨模态知识如何产生的训练动态。最后，作为概念验证，我们基于稀疏CLIP表征训练了一个视觉-语言模型，该模型支持可解释的、基于视觉的操控能力。我们的发现挑战了可解释性需要牺牲准确性的传统观念，并表明可解释性和性能可以共同优化，为未来的模型提供了一个有希望的设计原则。

title: Less is More: Clustered Cross-Covariance Control for Offline RL
summary: A fundamental challenge in offline reinforcement learning is distributional shift. Scarce data or datasets dominated by out-of-distribution (OOD) areas exacerbate this issue. Our theoretical analysis and experiments show that the standard squared error objective induces a harmful TD cross covariance. This effect amplifies in OOD areas, biasing optimization and degrading policy learning. To counteract this mechanism, we develop two complementary strategies: partitioned buffer sampling that restricts updates to localized replay partitions, attenuates irregular covariance effects, and aligns update directions, yielding a scheme that is easy to integrate with existing implementations, namely Clustered Cross-Covariance Control for TD (C^4). We also introduce an explicit gradient-based corrective penalty that cancels the covariance induced bias within each update. We prove that buffer partitioning preserves the lower bound property of the maximization objective, and that these constraints mitigate excessive conservatism in extreme OOD areas without altering the core behavior of policy constrained offline reinforcement learning. Empirically, our method showcases higher stability and up to 30% improvement in returns over prior methods, especially with small datasets and splits that emphasize OOD areas.
tldr: This paper introduces Clustered Cross-Covariance Control (C^4), a novel method for offline reinforcement learning that mitigates distributional shift issues by partitioning the replay buffer and applying gradient-based bias correction, resulting in improved stability and performance, especially with limited and out-of-distribution data.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了聚类交叉协方差控制（C^4），这是一种新的离线强化学习方法，通过划分回放缓冲区并应用基于梯度的偏差校正来缓解分布转移问题，从而提高稳定性和性能，特别是在数据有限和分布外数据的情况下。
summary_zh: 离线强化学习中的一个根本性挑战是分布偏移。稀缺的数据或被异于分布（OOD）区域主导的数据集会加剧这个问题。我们的理论分析和实验表明，标准的平方误差目标会诱导一种有害的TD交叉协方差。这种效应在OOD区域会放大，从而偏差优化并降低策略学习的效果。为了抵消这种机制，我们提出了两种互补策略：分区缓存采样，它将更新限制在本地化的回放分区中，衰减不规则的协方差效应，并对齐更新方向，从而产生一种易于与现有实现集成的方案，即用于TD的聚类交叉协方差控制（C^4）。我们还引入了一种显式的基于梯度的校正惩罚，以消除每次更新中由协方差引起的偏差。我们证明了缓冲区分割保留了最大化目标的下界属性，并且这些约束减轻了极端OOD区域中过度的保守性，而不会改变策略约束离线强化学习的核心行为。在实验中，我们的方法展现出更高的稳定性，并且相对于先前的方法，回报提高了高达30%，尤其是在小数据集和强调OOD区域的分割中。

title: Positive-Unlabeled Reinforcement Learning Distillation for On-Premise Small Models
summary: Due to constraints on privacy, cost, and latency, on-premise deployment of small models is increasingly common. However, most practical pipelines stop at supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and fail to reach the reinforcement learning (RL) alignment stage. The main reason is that RL alignment typically requires either expensive human preference annotation or heavy reliance on high-quality reward models with large-scale API usage and ongoing engineering maintenance, both of which are ill-suited to on-premise settings. To bridge this gap, we propose a positive-unlabeled (PU) RL distillation method for on-premise small-model deployment. Without human-labeled preferences or a reward model, our method distills the teacher's preference-optimization capability from black-box generations into a locally trainable student. For each prompt, we query the teacher once to obtain an anchor response, locally sample multiple student candidates, and perform anchor-conditioned self-ranking to induce pairwise or listwise preferences, enabling a fully local training loop via direct preference optimization or group relative policy optimization. Theoretical analysis justifies that the induced preference signal by our method is order-consistent and concentrates on near-optimal candidates, supporting its stability for preference optimization. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves consistently strong performance under a low-cost setting.
tldr: This paper presents a positive-unlabeled (PU) reinforcement learning distillation method for training small, on-premise language models without human preferences or a reward model by distilling the teacher's preference-optimization capability using self-ranking.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种正样本-无标签（PU）强化学习蒸馏方法，用于训练小型本地语言模型，无需人工偏好或奖励模型，通过使用自排序提炼教师的偏好优化能力。
summary_zh: 由于隐私、成本和延迟的限制，小型模型的本地部署正变得越来越普遍。然而，大多数实际的流程止步于监督式微调（SFT），未能达到强化学习（RL）对齐阶段。主要原因是RL对齐通常需要昂贵的人工偏好标注，或者严重依赖于高质量的奖励模型，而后者需要大规模的API使用和持续的工程维护，这两种方式都不适合本地部署环境。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了一种正样本-未标记样本（PU）的RL蒸馏方法，用于本地小型模型的部署。在没有人工标注偏好或奖励模型的情况下，我们的方法将教师模型的偏好优化能力从黑盒生成中提炼到本地可训练的学生模型中。对于每个提示，我们查询教师模型一次以获得一个锚定响应，在本地采样多个学生候选响应，并执行以锚定响应为条件的自排序，以诱导成对或列表式偏好，从而通过直接偏好优化或组相对策略优化实现完全本地的训练循环。理论分析表明，我们方法诱导的偏好信号在排序上是一致的，并且集中在接近最优的候选响应上，支持其偏好优化的稳定性。实验表明，我们的方法在低成本设置下实现了持续稳定的优异性能。

title: Spark: Strategic Policy-Aware Exploration via Dynamic Branching for Long-Horizon Agentic Learning
summary: Reinforcement learning has empowered large language models to act as intelligent agents, yet training them for long-horizon tasks remains challenging due to the scarcity of high-quality trajectories, especially under limited resources. Existing methods typically scale up rollout sizes and indiscriminately allocate computational resources among intermediate steps. Such attempts inherently waste substantial computation budget on trivial steps while failing to guarantee sample quality. To address this, we propose \textbf{Spark} (\textbf{S}trategic \textbf{P}olicy-\textbf{A}ware explo\textbf{R}ation via \textbf{K}ey-state dynamic branching), a novel framework that selectively branches at critical decision states for resource-efficient exploration. Our key insight is to activate adaptive branching exploration at critical decision points to probe promising trajectories, thereby achieving precise resource allocation that prioritizes sampling quality over blind coverage. This design leverages the agent's intrinsic decision-making signals to reduce dependence on human priors, enabling the agent to autonomously expand exploration and achieve stronger generalization. Experiments across diverse tasks (e.g., embodied planning), demonstrate that \textsc{Spark} achieves superior success rates with significantly fewer training samples, exhibiting robust generalization even in unseen scenarios.
tldr: The paper introduces Spark, a reinforcement learning framework that strategically allocates computational resources by dynamically branching at critical decision states to improve exploration efficiency and generalization in long-horizon tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为Spark的强化学习框架，它通过在关键决策状态下动态分支，策略性地分配计算资源，以提高长程任务中的探索效率和泛化能力。
summary_zh: 强化学习已赋能大型语言模型，使其能够像智能代理一样行动，但由于高质量轨迹的稀缺，尤其是在资源有限的情况下，训练它们完成长周期任务仍然充满挑战。现有方法通常扩大rollout规模，并在中间步骤中不加区分地分配计算资源。此类尝试本质上会在琐碎的步骤上浪费大量计算预算，同时无法保证样本质量。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新的框架\textbf{Spark}（\textbf{S}trategic \textbf{P}olicy-\textbf{A}ware explo\textbf{R}ation via \textbf{K}ey-state dynamic branching，通过关键状态动态分支进行策略感知战略探索），它有选择地在关键决策状态进行分支，以实现资源高效的探索。我们的核心洞察是，在关键决策点激活自适应分支探索来探测有前景的轨迹，从而实现精确的资源分配，优先考虑采样质量而不是盲目覆盖。这种设计利用了代理的内在决策信号，减少了对人类先验知识的依赖，使代理能够自主地扩展探索并实现更强的泛化能力。在各种任务（例如，具身规划）上的实验表明，\textsc{Spark}以显著更少的训练样本实现了更高的成功率，即使在未见过的场景中也表现出强大的泛化能力。

title: In-Context Reinforcement Learning From Suboptimal Historical Data
summary: Transformer models have achieved remarkable empirical successes, largely due to their in-context learning capabilities. Inspired by this, we explore training an autoregressive transformer for in-context reinforcement learning (ICRL). In this setting, we initially train a transformer on an offline dataset consisting of trajectories collected from various RL tasks, and then fix and use this transformer to create an action policy for new RL tasks. Notably, we consider the setting where the offline dataset contains trajectories sampled from suboptimal behavioral policies. In this case, standard autoregressive training corresponds to imitation learning and results in suboptimal performance. To address this, we propose the Decision Importance Transformer(DIT) framework, which emulates the actor-critic algorithm in an in-context manner. In particular, we first train a transformer-based value function that estimates the advantage functions of the behavior policies that collected the suboptimal trajectories. Then we train a transformer-based policy via a weighted maximum likelihood estimation loss, where the weights are constructed based on the trained value function to steer the suboptimal policies to the optimal ones. We conduct extensive experiments to test the performance of DIT on both bandit and Markov Decision Process problems. Our results show that DIT achieves superior performance, particularly when the offline dataset contains suboptimal historical data.
tldr: The paper introduces the Decision Importance Transformer (DIT) framework for in-context reinforcement learning from suboptimal historical data, effectively training a policy by weighting expert data based on an in-context actor-critic approach.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了决策重要性Transformer (DIT) 框架，用于从次优历史数据中进行上下文强化学习，通过基于上下文 Actor-Critic 方法对专家数据进行加权来有效地训练策略。
summary_zh: Transformer模型已取得了显著的实际成功，这很大程度上归功于它们的上下文学习能力。受此启发，我们探索训练一个用于上下文强化学习(ICRL)的自回归Transformer。在这种设置下，我们首先在一个由从各种RL任务收集的轨迹组成的离线数据集上训练一个Transformer，然后固定并使用该Transformer为新的RL任务创建动作策略。值得注意的是，我们考虑离线数据集包含从次优行为策略采样获得的轨迹的情况。在这种情况下，标准的自回归训练对应于模仿学习，并会导致次优的性能。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了决策重要性Transformer(DIT)框架，该框架以一种上下文的方式模拟了actor-critic算法。具体来说，我们首先训练一个基于Transformer的价值函数，该函数估计收集次优轨迹的行为策略的优势函数。然后，我们通过加权最大似然估计损失训练一个基于Transformer的策略，其中权重是基于训练好的价值函数构建的，以将次优策略引导到最优策略。我们进行了广泛的实验，以测试DIT在bandit和Markov决策过程问题上的性能。我们的结果表明，DIT取得了卓越的性能，尤其是在离线数据集包含次优历史数据时。

title: Self-Distillation Enables Continual Learning
summary: Continual learning, enabling models to acquire new skills and knowledge without degrading existing capabilities, remains a fundamental challenge for foundation models. While on-policy reinforcement learning can reduce forgetting, it requires explicit reward functions that are often unavailable. Learning from expert demonstrations, the primary alternative, is dominated by supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which is inherently off-policy. We introduce Self-Distillation Fine-Tuning (SDFT), a simple method that enables on-policy learning directly from demonstrations. SDFT leverages in-context learning by using a demonstration-conditioned model as its own teacher, generating on-policy training signals that preserve prior capabilities while acquiring new skills. Across skill learning and knowledge acquisition tasks, SDFT consistently outperforms SFT, achieving higher new-task accuracy while substantially reducing catastrophic forgetting. In sequential learning experiments, SDFT enables a single model to accumulate multiple skills over time without performance regression, establishing on-policy distillation as a practical path to continual learning from demonstrations.
tldr: The paper introduces Self-Distillation Fine-Tuning (SDFT) for continual learning from demonstrations, which leverages in-context learning to generate on-policy training signals, outperforming supervised fine-tuning in skill and knowledge acquisition while reducing catastrophic forgetting.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种用于从演示中持续学习的自蒸馏微调 (SDFT) 方法，该方法利用上下文学习来生成在线策略训练信号，在技能和知识获取方面优于监督微调，同时减少了灾难性遗忘。
summary_zh: 持续学习，使模型能够在不降低现有能力的情况下获取新技能和知识，仍然是基础模型面临的一项根本性挑战。虽然on-policy强化学习可以减少遗忘，但它需要通常不可用的显式奖励函数。从专家演示中学习，作为主要的替代方案，主要采用监督微调（SFT），而SFT本质上是off-policy的。我们引入了自蒸馏微调（SDFT），一种简单的可以直接从演示中实现on-policy学习的方法。 SDFT利用上下文学习，使用演示条件模型作为自身的教师，生成on-policy训练信号，在获取新技能的同时保持先前的能力。 在技能学习和知识获取任务中，SDFT始终优于SFT，在大幅减少灾难性遗忘的同时，实现了更高的新任务准确率。 在序贯学习实验中，SDFT使单个模型能够随着时间的推移积累多种技能而不会出现性能下降，从而将on-policy蒸馏确立为从演示中实现持续学习的实用途径。

title: GPO: Growing Policy Optimization for Legged Robot Locomotion and Whole-Body Control
summary: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies for legged robots remains challenging due to high-dimensional continuous actions, hardware constraints, and limited exploration. Existing methods for locomotion and whole-body control work well for position-based control with environment-specific heuristics (e.g., reward shaping, curriculum design, and manual initialization), but are less effective for torque-based control, where sufficiently exploring the action space and obtaining informative gradient signals for training is significantly more difficult. We introduce Growing Policy Optimization (GPO), a training framework that applies a time-varying action transformation to restrict the effective action space in the early stage, thereby encouraging more effective data collection and policy learning, and then progressively expands it to enhance exploration and achieve higher expected return. We prove that this transformation preserves the PPO update rule and introduces only bounded, vanishing gradient distortion, thereby ensuring stable training. We evaluate GPO on both quadruped and hexapod robots, including zero-shot deployment of simulation-trained policies on hardware. Policies trained with GPO consistently achieve better performance. These results suggest that GPO provides a general, environment-agnostic optimization framework for learning legged locomotion.
tldr: The paper introduces Growing Policy Optimization (GPO), a reinforcement learning framework for legged robot locomotion that uses a time-varying action transformation to improve exploration and performance, especially for torque-based control.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Growing Policy Optimization (GPO)，这是一个用于腿式机器人运动的强化学习框架，它使用随时间变化的动作转换来提高探索和性能，尤其是在基于扭矩的控制方面。
summary_zh: 由于高维连续动作空间、硬件约束和有限的探索能力，训练用于腿式机器人的强化学习(RL)策略仍然具有挑战性。现有的用于运动和全身控制的方法在基于位置的控制和环境特定的启发式方法（例如，奖励塑造、课程设计和手动初始化）方面表现良好，但在基于扭矩的控制方面效果较差，其中充分探索动作空间并获得用于训练的信息梯度信号难度显著增加。我们引入了增长策略优化（GPO），这是一种训练框架，它应用随时间变化的动作变换来限制早期阶段的有效动作空间，从而鼓励更有效的数据收集和策略学习，然后逐步扩展它以增强探索并获得更高的期望回报。我们证明了这种变换保留了PPO更新规则，并且仅引入有界的、消失的梯度失真，从而确保训练的稳定性。我们在四足机器人和六足机器人上评估了GPO，包括在硬件上对仿真训练的策略进行零样本部署。使用GPO训练的策略始终能获得更好的性能。这些结果表明，GPO为学习腿式运动提供了一个通用的、与环境无关的优化框架。

title: TRACER: Texture-Robust Affordance Chain-of-Thought for Deformable-Object Refinement
summary: The central challenge in robotic manipulation of deformable objects lies in aligning high-level semantic instructions with physical interaction points under complex appearance and texture variations. Due to near-infinite degrees of freedom, complex dynamics, and heterogeneous patterns, existing vision-based affordance prediction methods often suffer from boundary overflow and fragmented functional regions. To address these issues, we propose TRACER, a Texture-Robust Affordance Chain-of-thought with dEformable-object Refinement framework, which establishes a cross-hierarchical mapping from hierarchical semantic reasoning to appearance-robust and physically consistent functional region refinement. Specifically, a Tree-structured Affordance Chain-of-Thought (TA-CoT) is formulated to decompose high-level task intentions into hierarchical sub-task semantics, providing consistent guidance across various execution stages. To ensure spatial integrity, a Spatial-Constrained Boundary Refinement (SCBR) mechanism is introduced to suppress prediction spillover, guiding the perceptual response to converge toward authentic interaction manifolds. Furthermore, an Interactive Convergence Refinement Flow (ICRF) is developed to aggregate discrete pixels corrupted by appearance noise, significantly enhancing the spatial continuity and physical plausibility of the identified functional regions. Extensive experiments conducted on the Fine-AGDDO15 dataset and a real-world robotic platform demonstrate that TRACER significantly improves affordance grounding precision across diverse textures and patterns inherent to deformable objects. More importantly, it enhances the success rate of long-horizon tasks, effectively bridging the gap between high-level semantic reasoning and low-level physical execution. The source code and dataset will be made publicly available at https://github.com/Dikay1/TRACER.
tldr: TRACER is a novel framework for robotic manipulation of deformable objects that combines a Tree-structured Affordance Chain-of-Thought with Spatial-Constrained Boundary Refinement and Interactive Convergence Refinement Flow to improve affordance grounding and long-horizon task success in the presence of texture variations.
tldr_zh: TRACER是一个用于机器人操作可变形对象的新框架，它结合了树状结构的功能链式思考（Tree-structured Affordance Chain-of-Thought）与空间约束边界细化（Spatial-Constrained Boundary Refinement）和交互式收敛细化流（Interactive Convergence Refinement Flow），从而在存在纹理变化的情况下，提高功能定位和长时程任务的成功率。
summary_zh: 对可变形物体进行机器人操作的核心挑战在于，如何在复杂的外观和纹理变化下，将高层语义指令与物理交互点对齐。由于近乎无限的自由度、复杂的动力学和异质模式，现有的基于视觉的直接性预测方法常常面临边界溢出和碎片化的功能区域问题。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了TRACER，一个具有可变形物体细化的纹理鲁棒的直接性思维链框架，它建立了一个从分层语义推理到外观鲁棒且物理一致的功能区域细化的跨层级映射。具体来说，我们构建了一个树状结构的直接性思维链（TA-CoT），将高层任务意图分解为分层的子任务语义，为各个执行阶段提供一致的指导。为了确保空间完整性，我们引入了一个空间约束的边界细化（SCBR）机制来抑制预测溢出，引导感知响应收敛到真实的交互流形。此外，我们开发了一个交互式收敛细化流（ICRF）来聚合被外观噪声破坏的离散像素，显著增强了已识别功能区域的空间连续性和物理合理性。在Fine-AGDDO15数据集和一个真实机器人平台上进行的大量实验表明，TRACER显著提高了可变形物体固有的多样纹理和模式下的直接性定位精度。更重要的是，它提高了长时程任务的成功率，有效地弥合了高层语义推理和低层物理执行之间的差距。源代码和数据集将在https://github.com/Dikay1/TRACER上公开提供。

title: Game-Theoretic Autonomous Driving: A Graphs of Convex Sets Approach
summary: Multi-vehicle autonomous driving couples strategic interaction with hybrid (discrete-continuous) maneuver planning under shared safety constraints.
